sheets.name: Product & Launch Activities
rows: Activity/Offer
Description
AE Actions
Target Segments
Who to Invite/Engage
Expected Outcome
Best Timing
New Product Launch
Major new product or feature release with market announcement
Create personalized launch briefs for top accounts. Schedule product demos. Build business case showing how new product solves their specific challenges. Offer early access or beta participation.
Existing customers (expansion), Strategic prospects (new logo), Accounts with known pain point this solves
Product champions, Technical decision-makers, Department heads who own budget for this product category, Innovation-focused executives
15-30% increase in demo requests, 3-5 expansion opportunities, 10-15 qualified new prospects
Week of launch + 2 weeks after
Feature Release
Incremental feature or enhancement to existing product
Email customers using related features. Offer training webinar. Schedule QBRs to discuss upgrade path. Demo premium tier features.
Existing customers (current tier users), Freemium/trial users ready to convert, Churned customers who left due to missing feature
Power users of related features, Product admins, Customer success champions who requested this feature
10-20% feature adoption, 5-10% tier upgrades, 2-3 win-backs
Within 2 weeks of release
Beta/Early Access Program
Exclusive access to new capabilities before general release
Invite strategic accounts and product champions. Position as exclusive opportunity. Gather feedback and build case studies. Offer pricing incentive for early adopters.
Strategic accounts (high revenue or logo value), Product champions and advocates, Design partner prospects, Innovator/early adopter profiles
Technical champions, Product managers, Forward-thinking executives, Users who have submitted feature requests
8-12 beta participants, 3-5 design partner case studies, 60-80% beta-to-paid conversion
3-6 months before GA launch
Product Roadmap Session
Private briefing on future product direction and strategy
Schedule exclusive roadmap reviews with top accounts. Gather strategic feedback. Align product direction with customer needs. Build long-term partnership.
Top 20% revenue accounts, Strategic logo accounts, At-risk accounts (retention play), Prospects in late-stage evaluation
C-suite and VP-level executives, Product leadership, Innovation teams, Technical architects
90%+ retention of at-risk accounts, 3-5 upsell commitments, 2-3 closed late-stage deals
Quarterly or bi-annually
Customer Advisory Board
Formal group of customers who provide strategic product input
Recruit executive sponsors from top accounts. Facilitate quarterly meetings. Provide exclusive access to executives and roadmap. Build deep partnership.
Top 10% revenue customers, Strategic logo/reference accounts, Industry thought leaders, Customers with sophisticated use cases
C-suite executives, SVPs/VPs who own strategy, Product leadership with decision authority
Lifetime value increase of 40-60%, 100% retention rate, 5-8 strong reference accounts
Ongoing quarterly meetings
Integration Launch
New integration with partner platform (Salesforce, Slack, etc)
Target accounts using partner platform. Offer joint implementation support. Co-market with partner. Build integration-specific business case.
Accounts using partner platform, Prospects evaluating partner ecosystem, Accounts who requested this integration
Technical admins of partner platform, Integration/IT teams, Business owners who use both platforms
20-30% increase in qualified pipeline, 10-15% faster sales cycles, 5-10 expansion deals
Launch week + 4 weeks after
Pricing/Packaging Change
New pricing tiers, packaging updates, or promotional pricing
Grandfather existing customers or offer loyalty pricing. Reach out to stalled deals with new packaging. Target high-usage freemium accounts with conversion offer.
High-usage accounts on lower tiers, Stalled deals with pricing objections, Freemium users exceeding limits, Renewal accounts (bundling opportunity)
Economic buyers, Procurement/finance, Department heads with budget authority, CFO (for enterprise deals)
15-25% conversion rate on targeted offers, 10-15% expansion rate, 20-30% deal acceleration
Immediately upon change
Product Certification/Training
Formal training program leading to user certification
Offer to customer champions and power users. Position as career development. Create certified user community. Leverage for account penetration.
Existing customers with low adoption, New customers in onboarding, Champions you want to develop, Prospects evaluating (technical validation)
End users and practitioners, Customer success/admins, Technical champions, New hires in customer accounts
30-40% increase in product adoption, 5-10 new champions developed, 80%+ certification completion
Ongoing monthly cohorts

sheets.name: Events & Experiences
rows: Activity/Offer
Description
AE Actions
Target Segments
Who to Invite/Engage
Expected Outcome
Best Timing
Executive Dinner (Intimate)
Small private dinner (8-20 executives) focused on specific topic
Curate guest list of complementary executives. Prepare personalized invitations. Create seating chart for strategic conversations. Follow up within 48 hours with specific next steps.
C-suite and VP targets from top accounts, Late-stage prospects with executive buying committee, Strategic prospects in specific vertical/geography, Expansion targets (new departments)
C-suite (CEO, CFO, CRO, CMO, CTO), SVPs and VPs with P&L ownership, Board members or advisors, Industry influencers
35-50% follow-up meeting acceptance, $1-3M pipeline per event, 3-5 qualified opportunities, 1-2 closed deals within 90 days
Evening (Tue-Thu), avoid Monday/Friday, advance notice 3-4 weeks
User Conference (Annual)
Large-scale customer conference with training, networking, product updates
Book 1-on-1 meetings with top accounts. Schedule executive business reviews. Host VIP dinner for strategic accounts. Staff booth for new prospect meetings. Conduct expansion workshops.
All existing customers (engagement/retention), Strategic prospects (invite as guests), Partners and ecosystem (co-selling), Churned customers (win-back)
User conference: All customer contacts, VIP track: C-suite and VP execs, Training sessions: Practitioners and admins, Partner summit: Channel/tech partners
80-100 qualified expansion opportunities, $5-15M pipeline, 90%+ customer satisfaction, 20-30 customer case studies, 50-100 prospect meetings
Annually, typically spring or fall
Industry Conference (Sponsor)
Third-party conference where you sponsor/exhibit
Pre-book meetings with target accounts attending. Host hospitality suite or dinner. Staff booth strategically. Coordinate speaking sessions. Run targeted ads to attendees pre-event.
Target accounts registered to attend, Prospects in conference geography, Customers attending (retention/expansion), Competitive accounts (displacement)
Decision-makers in target accounts, Industry influencers and analysts, Prospects researching solutions, Existing customers (schedule QBRs)
100-150 qualified conversations, 30-50 qualified opportunities, $3-8M pipeline, 10-20 customer meetings
Pre-event: 4-6 weeks before, during event: maximize booth traffic, post-event: follow up within 1 week
Webinar (Educational)
Online educational session on industry topic or best practices
Promote to target account lists. Identify attendees from target accounts. Follow up with recording + personalized outreach. Offer 1-on-1 consultation for attendees.
Early-stage prospects (awareness), Existing customers (thought leadership), Freemium users (conversion), Cold accounts (warming play)
Mid-level practitioners and managers, Technical evaluators, Department heads seeking education, Analysts/influencers
200-500 registrations, 40-50% attendance, 15-20% follow-up meeting acceptance, 10-20 qualified leads
Ongoing monthly, Wed-Thu 10am-2pm local time
Executive Roundtable
Facilitated discussion among non-competing executives on strategic topic
Invite 10-15 executives to discuss peer challenges. Facilitate (don't pitch). Build relationships through value-add. Position as exclusive network.
C-suite and VP targets (similar roles, non-competing), Strategic accounts you want access to, Existing executive champions (bring peers), Prospects researching strategic initiatives
C-suite in similar roles (all CMOs, all CFOs), SVPs/VPs with strategic mandate, Innovation leaders, Industry veterans/influencers
60-70% attendance rate, 5-8 follow-up meetings, Deep relationship building, 2-3 warm referrals per attendee
Quarterly, 90-minute morning or lunch format
Lunch & Learn
Casual lunch presentation at customer/prospect office
Schedule with customer champion or prospect contact. Present relevant best practices or product training. Bring lunch for team. Low-pressure relationship building.
Existing customers (expand into new dept), Warm prospects with internal champion, Accounts evaluating (stakeholder expansion), New customer onboarding
Department teams (8-20 people), Cross-functional stakeholders, End users and practitioners, Mid-level managers
Direct access to 10-20 stakeholders, 3-5 new champion relationships, 30-40% increase in product awareness, 1-2 expansion opportunities
Monthly at customer sites, lunch hours 11:30am-1pm
Workshop/Training Session
Hands-on training or strategic workshop (half-day or full-day)
Position as value-add for customers or high-intent prospects. Customize to their specific use case. Bring subject matter experts. Create actionable deliverables.
Existing customers with low adoption, New customers in onboarding phase, Late-stage prospects (technical validation), Expansion accounts (new use case)
Power users and admins, Implementation teams, Department heads overseeing rollout, Technical champions
80-90% completion rate, 40-50% increase in product adoption, 2-3 expansion opportunities identified, Strong champion development
Quarterly for customers, during sales cycle for prospects
Virtual Executive Briefing
1-hour video call with your executives and prospect/customer executives
Position as exclusive access to leadership. Align on strategic priorities. Discuss partnership vision. Address executive-level concerns.
Late-stage prospects with executive buying committee, Strategic accounts at renewal, At-risk accounts (executive save), Expansion opportunities requiring exec buy-in
C-suite (CEO, CFO, CRO), Board members, SVPs with strategic mandate, Economic buyers
70-80% deal acceleration, 50-60% close rate increase, Executive relationship established, 2-3 objections resolved
During active sales cycle or at-risk period
Customer Advisory Board Meeting
Quarterly in-person meeting of CAB members
Host at company HQ or destination location. Provide roadmap sneak peek. Facilitate peer discussion. Recognize member contributions. Gather strategic product feedback.
CAB members (top strategic accounts)
C-suite and SVP/VP from member companies, Your C-suite and product leadership, Selected product managers
100% retention of CAB accounts, 3-5 strategic product insights, Deep executive relationships, Co-marketing/speaking opportunities
Quarterly, 1-2 day format
Hospitality Suite (Conference)
Private room at conference for meetings and networking
Pre-book 20-30 meetings with targets. Offer refuge from conference floor. Provide food/beverage. Create intimate networking environment.
Target accounts attending conference, Strategic prospects in area, Customers attending (QBRs), Partners for co-selling discussions
All levels: Execs for strategic discussions, practitioners for demos, partners for planning
40-60 quality conversations, 15-20 qualified opportunities, Stronger relationship building than booth, Partner collaboration
During multi-day conference, hours extend beyond show floor

sheets.name: Content & Thought Leadership
rows: Activity/Offer
Description
AE Actions
Target Segments
Who to Invite/Engage
Expected Outcome
Best Timing
Industry Research Report
Original research with data and insights on industry trends
Share with target accounts as conversation starter. Offer briefing on findings. Position as thought leader. Use in prospecting cadences.
C-suite and VP targets (strategic), Industry analysts and influencers, Prospects researching market, All existing customers (value-add)
C-suite and VPs (strategic insights), Directors/managers (tactical insights), Analysts/press (amplification), Customer champions (share internally)
500-2000 downloads, 15-20% meeting acceptance from outreach, Media coverage and brand awareness, 30-50 qualified conversations
Annually or bi-annually, promote for 3-6 months
Custom ROI Assessment
Personalized analysis of ROI/business impact for prospect
Offer to qualified prospects in evaluation. Gather their specific data/assumptions. Build custom business case. Present findings to buying committee.
Late-stage prospects with business case step, Prospects with ROI/pricing concerns, Economic buyer engaged (CFO, VP Finance), Enterprise deals requiring board approval
Economic buyers (CFO, VP Finance), Business case owner/champion, C-suite (final approval), Procurement (validation)
60-70% deal acceleration, 40-50% increase in close rate, Larger deal sizes (quantified value), Reduced discounting pressure
Mid-to-late stage sales cycle
Benchmark Report
Comparative data showing how companies perform vs peers
Offer personalized benchmarking to prospects/customers. Show gap analysis. Position solution as way to close performance gaps. Create urgency through comparison.
Prospects in evaluation (competitive pressure), Existing customers with low adoption, Executive sponsors needing justification, Competitive displacement targets
C-suite and VPs (strategic metrics), Department heads (operational metrics), Analysts and FP&A (validation), Champions building internal case
40-50% of recipients request follow-up, Creates urgency (peer comparison), 2-3x increase in engagement, Expansion justification for customers
Quarterly or bi-annually
Case Study/Success Story
Detailed customer story with metrics and outcomes
Share with similar prospects (industry, size, use case). Offer reference call with featured customer. Use in proposals and sales collateral. Post on website and promote.
Prospects in similar industry/segment, Prospects with similar use case/challenge, Late-stage prospects needing social proof, Competitive situations (vs competitor)
Decision-makers in similar role as case study subject, Technical evaluators (implementation details), C-suite (strategic validation), Procurement (vendor validation)
30-40% increase in conversion when shared, Strong social proof asset, Shortens sales cycle by 15-20%, Overcomes 'risk of new vendor' objection
Ongoing, refresh every 6-12 months
Analyst Report Inclusion
Gartner, Forrester, or other analyst recognition
Share report with target accounts. Offer analyst briefing for key prospects. Use in competitive situations. Position as category leader/challenger.
Enterprise prospects requiring analyst validation, Procurement teams doing vendor research, C-suite evaluating strategic vendors, Competitive displacement opportunities
C-suite and SVPs (strategic vendor decisions), Procurement/sourcing (validation), IT/technical evaluators (due diligence), Risk management/compliance
40-50% increase in enterprise pipeline, Credibility with large accounts, 20-30% shorter enterprise sales cycles, Competitive differentiation
When report publishes, use for 12 months
Executive Blog/Byline
Thought leadership article by your executive in industry publication
Share with target accounts. Offer to discuss topic. Position executive for speaking/advisory. Use in relationship-building outreach.
C-suite and VP prospects (peer-level), Industry influencers and analysts, Strategic accounts (executive relationship), Media and press contacts
C-suite (peer engagement), VPs with strategic mandate, Industry associations/groups, Prospects researching thought leaders
Brand awareness and credibility, Executive relationship building, 10-15% increase in executive engagement, Speaking/advisory invitations
Ongoing quarterly, tie to industry events or trends
Video Demo/Walkthrough
Personalized video demo created for specific prospect
Record custom demo addressing prospect's specific use case. Reference their company and challenges. Send via personalized email. Follow up within 48 hours.
Active prospects in demo stage, Inbound leads needing qualification, Stalled opportunities (re-engagement), Expansion opportunities (new use case)
Primary decision-maker, Technical evaluator, Business case owner, Implementation team if identified
60-70% video view rate, 30-40% follow-up meeting acceptance, Demonstrates customization/care, 2-3x higher response than generic demo
Immediately after discovery call or demo request
Customer Webinar/Panel
Existing customers present on their success and best practices
Recruit strong customer advocates. Promote to prospects in similar industries. Facilitate panel discussion. Offer to connect attendees with panelists.
Prospects in same industry as panelists, Prospects with similar use case, Mid-stage evaluation (need social proof), Existing customers (engagement)
Decision-makers in similar roles, Technical evaluators (implementation), Department heads (use case validation), Customer champions (networking)
300-600 registrations, 45-50% attendance, 20-25% follow-up meeting acceptance, Strong social proof and credibility
Monthly or quarterly
Product Comparison Guide
Objective comparison of your product vs competitors
Share with prospects in competitive evaluation. Address objections proactively. Offer demo to show differentiation. Position unique value props.
Prospects in active bake-off, Accounts using competitor (displacement), Prospects researching category, Customers considering competitor (retention)
Primary evaluators/decision-makers, Technical champions, Procurement (vendor evaluation), Economic buyers (value comparison)
50-60% win rate in competitive situations, Addresses objections before they arise, 15-20% faster decision-making, Differentiation clarity
During competitive evaluation phase
Implementation Guide/Playbook
Best practices guide for successful implementation
Share with late-stage prospects to reduce perceived risk. Offer to walk through approach. Position as de-risking purchase. Share customer success metrics.
Late-stage prospects with implementation concerns, Prospects evaluating (technical validation), New customers in contract negotiation, Expansion accounts (new department)
Implementation/operations teams, Technical decision-makers, Change management leads, Executive sponsor (risk mitigation)
30-40% reduction in 'implementation risk' objection, 15-20% faster deal closure, Confidence building asset, Post-sale: 25-30% faster time-to-value
Late-stage sales cycle and onboarding

sheets.name: Personalization & Gifting
rows: Activity/Offer
Description
AE Actions
Target Segments
Who to Invite/Engage
Expected Outcome
Best Timing
Executive Gift (High-Value)
Premium gift for C-suite prospects/customers ($200-500)
Research recipient interests. Send personalized note tied to business conversation. Follow up after delivery. No strings attached value-add.
C-suite targets in active deals, Top customer executives at renewal, Executive champions you want to strengthen, Key executives at strategic logo targets
C-suite only (CEO, CFO, CRO, CMO, CTO), Board members if relevant, Executive champions with significant influence
60-70% acknowledgment/thank you, 40-50% follow-up meeting acceptance, Relationship deepening, Differentiation from competitors
After significant milestone, holiday season, contract signing
Personalized Gift (Mid-Value)
Thoughtful gift based on individual research ($50-150)
Use social/LinkedIn research to find interests. Send with personalized note. Reference specific conversation or shared interest. Follow up within week.
Key decision-makers in active deals, Champions and advocates, Influencers in buying committee, Customer success managers/admins
VPs and Directors, Key champions and coaches, Technical decision-makers, Customer admins/power users
70-80% acknowledgment rate, 30-40% response/engagement increase, Memorable touchpoint, Relationship building
After discovery call, mid-deal milestone, customer success
Company Swag Box
Branded merchandise box with useful items
Send to new customers at kickoff. Send to prospects after demo. Include personalized welcome note. Mix of practical and premium items.
New customers (onboarding welcome), Post-demo prospects (qualified), Event attendees (follow-up), Customer team expansion (new users)
New customer contacts (all levels), Qualified prospects post-demo, Event attendees, New team members at accounts
High brand recall, Practical daily-use items, 15-20% social media sharing, Team-building at customer site
New customer: week 1, prospects: post-demo, events: immediately after
Book with Personalized Note
Relevant business book with handwritten note explaining why
Choose book relevant to their role/challenges. Write meaningful note connecting book to their business. Reference in follow-up call. Ship directly.
C-suite and VP prospects, Champions building internal case, Customer executives at QBR, Prospects in long sales cycle (nurture)
C-suite and VPs (strategic books), Directors/managers (tactical books), Champions (career development), Thought leaders (industry books)
70-80% appreciation/acknowledgment, Thoughtful differentiation, 20-30% follow-up conversation rate, Long-term relationship investment
After strategic conversation, holiday season, career milestones
Local Experience Gift
Restaurant gift card, event tickets, local experience
Research local to their office/city. Time to personal milestone if known. Send with note suggesting use. Creates positive association with your brand.
Key contacts in active deals, Champions helping you navigate account, Customer advocates (thank you), Prospects in long sales cycle
All levels based on appropriateness, Decision-makers and champions, Customer success champions, Executive assistants (gatekeepers)
90-95% redemption rate, Positive brand association, 25-30% relationship strengthening, Gatekeeper access improvement
After they help you, holiday season, upon learning of milestone
Handwritten Note
Personal handwritten card (no gift attached)
Write after meeting, call, or milestone. Reference specific conversation detail. Thank them or congratulate them. Mail physically (not email).
All prospects after meaningful interaction, Customers after QBR or success, New contacts after introduction, After win/promotion/company milestone
All levels appropriate for note, Particularly impactful for executives, New champions, Long-term relationship contacts
Unexpected touchpoint stands out, 40-50% strengthens relationship perception, Differentiation from competitors, Low-cost high-impact
After meetings, customer wins, personal milestones, thank yous
Coffee/Meal Gift Card
Starbucks, Doordash, local restaurant gift card ($25-50)
Send as thank-you for taking meeting. Include note suggesting coffee chat. Low-pressure relationship building. Quick acknowledgment of their time.
Prospects after discovery calls, Referral sources who introduce you, Lower-level champions and coaches, Customer users who provide feedback
Mid-level contacts (managers, directors), Champions and coaches, Referral sources, Customer power users providing input
High redemption rate (85-90%), Appreciated gesture for time, 20-25% increases responsiveness, Inexpensive relationship maintenance
Immediately after meeting, after referral, after customer feedback
Charitable Donation in Their Name
Donation to cause they care about instead of gift
Research causes they support (LinkedIn, company site). Make donation. Send certificate/notification. Aligns with their values.
Executives who prefer no gifts, Prospects at companies with strict gift policies, Cause-driven executives and companies, Holiday season alternative to gifting
C-suite and VPs (values alignment), Executives at non-profits/education, Government/public sector (gift restrictions), ESG-focused contacts
100% compliance with gift policies, Values-based relationship building, 50-60% deepens relationship/appreciation, Differentiation through values
Holiday season, after contract signing, upon learning of cause they support
Virtual Event Access
Ticket to virtual conference, course, or certification
Gift access to relevant virtual event/training. Position as career development investment. Follow up to discuss learnings. Shows investment in them personally.
Champions you're developing, Technical evaluators (certifications), Mid-level contacts (career development), Customer advocates (reward)
Mid-level practitioners and managers, Technical champions, Customer success/admin contacts, Emerging leaders you're cultivating
High perceived value (career investment), 60-70% completion rate, Relationship deepening, Potential for them to advocate for you
When relevant event announced, after they help you, career development milestones
Custom Video Message
Personalized video from your executive to theirs
Record CEO/executive thanking prospect/customer. Reference specific conversation or milestone. Send at strategic moment. High-touch personal approach.
C-suite prospects in final stages, Top customer renewals, At-risk strategic accounts, Deal closed celebration
C-suite to C-suite, Your exec to their exec sponsor, Executive buying committee, Customer executive champions
95%+ view rate, Significant differentiation, 40-50% accelerates decision, Executive relationship strengthened
Final stages of deal, customer milestone, at-risk save, closed deal thank you

sheets.name: Customer Marketing & Advocacy
rows: Activity/Offer
Description
AE Actions
Target Segments
Who to Invite/Engage
Expected Outcome
Best Timing
Customer Reference Program
Formal program where customers provide references for prospects
Recruit satisfied customers. Provide incentives (swag, credits, recognition). Match references to similar prospects. Track and thank participants.
Highly satisfied customers (NPS 9-10), Customers with measurable results, Logo/brand name customers, Industry-specific success stories
Customer champions and advocates, Executives willing to speak peer-to-peer, Technical leaders (implementation references), Department heads (use case references)
15-25 active references, 40-50% close rate when reference provided, Shortened sales cycles (15-20%), Reduced risk perception
Ongoing program, recruit after 6+ months of success
Customer Case Study Program
Create detailed case studies with customer metrics
Identify customers with strong results. Offer incentives for participation. Work with marketing to create. Promote customer's brand/leader. Share draft for approval.
Customers with quantifiable results, Strategic logo customers, Customers with unique use cases, Industry-specific reference needs
Customer executive sponsors, Department heads owning initiative, Champions who drove adoption, Marketing contacts (co-promotion)
8-12 case studies annually, Strong sales enablement asset, Customer relationship deepening, Co-marketing opportunities
After 6-12 months of measurable success
Customer Award/Recognition
Formal recognition of customer innovation, results, or partnership
Nominate top customers for awards. Create 'Customer of the Year' program. Recognize at user conference. Promote their success publicly.
Top performing customers, Innovative use case customers, Strong advocates and champions, Strategic logo accounts
Customer executives (public recognition), Department heads (career benefit), Champions (advocate development), Marketing (co-promotion)
Deep customer loyalty, 80-90% retention of recognized accounts, Advocacy development, Lifetime value increase of 30-40%
Annually at user conference or quarterly
Customer Advisory Board
Exclusive group providing strategic product/market input
Recruit top strategic accounts. Facilitate quarterly meetings. Provide roadmap access. Treat as strategic partners. Compensation: influence, access, networking.
Top 10% revenue accounts, Strategic logo/industry leader accounts, Sophisticated use case customers, Customers with strategic vision
C-suite executives, SVPs/VPs with strategic mandate, Product leadership, Innovation/strategy leaders
12-20 CAB members, 100% retention rate, Strategic product insights, Deep executive relationships, Co-marketing/speaking
Ongoing quarterly meetings
Customer Co-Marketing
Joint marketing activities (webinar, content, event)
Identify customers willing to co-market. Develop joint value prop. Create co-branded content. Promote to both audiences. Share leads with AE.
Customers with compelling stories, Industry thought leader customers, Customers seeking brand exposure, Strategic partnerships
Customer marketing leaders, Department heads with story to tell, Executives building personal brand, Product champions
Both brands benefit from exposure, Qualified leads from customer's audience, Case study content created, Relationship deepening
After measurable success achieved
Customer Review Incentive
Program to encourage G2, Gartner Peer Insights reviews
Request reviews from satisfied customers. Provide easy process/templates. Offer incentive (gift card, donation). Thank and recognize participants.
Satisfied customers (NPS 8-10), Customers past onboarding (3+ months), Power users with strong opinions, Renewal accounts (engagement)
Champions and advocates, Power users and admins, Department heads with opinion, Technical users (product reviews)
20-40 reviews annually, Improved review site rankings, Social proof for prospects, Customer engagement touchpoint
After customer success milestone, quarterly campaigns
Customer Exclusive Access
Early access to features, beta programs, executive briefings
Offer exclusive access to top customers. Provide roadmap previews. Invite to executive briefings. Create VIP tier experience.
Top revenue customers, Strategic logo accounts, At-risk customers (retention play), Product champion accounts
Executive sponsors, Product leadership at customer, Power users and admins, Innovation/strategy teams
High perceived value, 90-95% retention of VIP accounts, Product feedback and insights, Advocacy development
Ongoing throughout year
Customer Success Story Video
Professional video showcasing customer results
Identify customers with visual/compelling stories. Produce professional video. Feature customer executives. Promote across channels. Share with prospects.
Customers with strong visual story, Industry leader/logo customers, Customers with measurable impact, Customers seeking exposure
Customer executives (personal brand building), Department heads owning initiative, Marketing teams (co-promotion), End users (testimonials)
High-value sales asset, 40-50% increase in engagement when shared, Customer appreciation, Multiple use cases (social, web, sales)
After significant results achieved
Customer Community Access
Online community for customers to connect and share
Invite customers to exclusive community. Facilitate peer connections. Surface customer challenges. Identify expansion opportunities through engagement.
All customers (engagement/retention), Champions (develop into advocates), Customers with low adoption (support), New customers (onboarding)
All customer contacts, Particularly: admins and power users, Champions building peer network, New users seeking help
70-80% customer engagement, Peer-to-peer support (reduces support costs), Adoption improvement (15-25%), Expansion opportunity identification
Ongoing, invite at onboarding
Customer QBR/EBR
Quarterly or executive business review with customer
Schedule with strategic accounts. Review usage, ROI, and success metrics. Identify expansion opportunities. Address concerns proactively. Bring in executives if strategic.
Top 20-30% revenue accounts, At-risk accounts, Renewal accounts (90 days prior), Expansion target accounts
QBR: Department heads, champions, admins; EBR: C-suite and VPs, executive sponsors, procurement
Identify 2-3 expansion opportunities per QBR, Address concerns before escalate, 85-95% retention rate, Executive relationship building
Quarterly for top accounts, annually for others

sheets.name: Sales Enablement Plays
rows: Activity/Offer
Description
AE Actions
Target Segments
Who to Invite/Engage
Expected Outcome
Best Timing
Free Trial/Freemium
Limited-time or feature-limited free product access
Identify high-intent signups. Monitor usage and engagement. Trigger outreach based on behavior. Offer upgrade path with clear value.
Freemium: All inbound signups; Trial: Qualified prospects in evaluation, Small businesses (product-led growth), Technical evaluators needing hands-on
Trials: Decision-makers and evaluators; Freemium: End users (bottoms-up adoption), Technical champions, Individual contributors
10-25% trial-to-paid conversion, Reduced sales cycle (self-education), Product-qualified leads (PQLs), 30-40% faster evaluation
Immediate for trials, freemium ongoing
POC/Pilot Program
Structured proof-of-concept with success criteria
Define clear success criteria with customer. Scope limited timeframe (30-60 days). Provide dedicated support. Measure and report on results. Convert to paid based on proven value.
Late-stage prospects requiring validation, Enterprise accounts with lengthy evaluations, Risk-averse buyers, Technical validation required
Technical evaluators and admins, Department head (use case owner), Champions (internal advocates), Economic buyer (final approval)
60-70% POC-to-paid conversion, De-risks purchase decision, Builds internal champions during POC, Faster post-POC closure
Late-stage evaluation, after vendor shortlist
ROI/Business Case Workshop
Collaborative session to build financial justification
Facilitate workshop with customer stakeholders. Gather their data and assumptions. Build custom ROI model. Present to buying committee or executives.
Mid-to-late stage prospects, Enterprise deals requiring CFO approval, Deals stalled on business case, Champions needing justification tools
Champions building internal case, Finance/FP&A teams, Economic buyers (CFO, VP Finance), Department heads with budget
40-50% deal acceleration, Larger deal sizes (quantified value), 60-70% close rate improvement, Reduces discounting pressure
Mid-stage when business case discussion begins
Account Mapping Session
Internal session to map account org structure and strategy
Use LinkedIn Sales Nav and tools to map org. Identify decision-makers and influencers. Plot relationships and champions. Develop multi-threaded strategy.
Strategic target accounts, Enterprise accounts with complex buying, Accounts where you have one contact, Expansion accounts (map new departments)
Internal: AE, SDR, manager, solutions engineer; External intel: From champions, from LinkedIn, from tools
Multi-threaded account strategy, 30-40% increase in executive access, Reduced single-threader risk, Faster deal progression
Early in account engagement, quarterly review for strategic accounts
Competitive Displacement Play
Systematic approach to displace incumbent vendor
Research incumbent weaknesses. Identify contract renewal date. Build compelling business case for switching. Account for switching costs in ROI. Align to new executive if possible.
Accounts using competitor, Competitors with known weaknesses, Accounts with new executives (vendor review), Post-M&A accounts (consolidation)
New executives (fresh perspective), Dissatisfied users of competitor, Champions who prefer your solution, Procurement (vendor review cycles)
15-20% displacement success rate, Higher deal values (full replacement), Long sales cycles (8-12 months), Deep discovery required
3-6 months before competitor contract renewal
Expansion/Land-and-Expand Play
Systematic approach to expand from initial department
Start with quick win in one department. Deliver fast value. Build champion. Use success to expand to adjacent departments. Target department heads in expansion areas.
Existing customers with successful deployment, Customers with single-department usage, Customers with multiple departments matching ICP, Customers with new use cases emerging
Initial: Existing champion and users; Expansion: New department heads, New use case owners, Cross-functional leaders
60-70% of customers expand within 12 months, 2-3x increase in account value, Reduced CAC for expansion, Higher retention (multi-dept)
After initial success (6-12 months)
Executive Sponsorship Play
Secure C-suite or VP sponsor for strategic initiative
Position solution as strategic vs tactical. Connect to business objectives. Provide executive-level content and briefings. Multi-thread but drive executive relationship.
Enterprise strategic deals, Deals requiring cross-functional buy-in, Transformation/change management initiatives, Strategic partnerships
C-suite (CEO, CFO, CRO, CTO), SVPs with strategic mandate, Board members if relevant, Executive steering committees
Executive sponsor increases close rate 40-50%, Faster decision-making, Reduced organizational resistance, Higher deal values
Early in enterprise sales cycle
Champion Development Play
Identify and develop internal champion at prospect
Identify potential champion (user with influence). Provide tools to build internal case. Arm with ROI data and competitive intel. Coach them on navigating org. Offer career development value.
All prospects (need internal advocate), Complex organizations (need guide), Consensus buying situations, Accounts where you lack executive access
Mid-level managers with influence, Power users who love product, Department heads with initiative ownership, Rising stars with exec relationships
Champions increase close rate 3-4x, Provide internal intelligence, Navigate politics and process, Sell on your behalf when you're not there
Early-to-mid sales cycle
Renewal Play (At-Risk)
Systematic approach to save at-risk renewal
Identify risk signals early (usage decline, support issues, stakeholder changes). Escalate internally. Schedule executive business review. Address root causes. Provide concessions if needed.
Customers approaching renewal with risk signals, Customers with usage decline, Customers with new executives, Customers with budget cuts
Executive sponsors at customer, Department heads using product, Customer success/admin contacts, New executives if stakeholder change
70-80% at-risk save rate, May require concessions, Executive engagement critical, Address root cause not symptom
90 days before renewal, earlier if risk signals
Upsell Play (Expansion)
Systematic approach to sell premium tiers or add-ons
Monitor usage approaching tier limits. Track feature requests for premium features. Demonstrate ROI of current tier. Build business case for upgrade. Offer trial of premium features.
Customers on lower tiers with high usage, Customers requesting premium features, Successful customers ready for more, Customers with expanding teams
Department heads with budget authority, Power users wanting premium features, Executive sponsors for large expansions, Procurement for contract amendments
30-40% upsell success rate within customer base, 50-70% increase in account value, Lower friction than new sales, Faster sales cycles
After customer success established (6+ months)
